channel.title: How can I add Frontity to an existing react project?
channel.description:
I have developed a decent sized react project already for my website. All the documentation that I have found so far, assumes that I'm creating my react project with Frontity. Would it be possible to add Frontity framework to it by installing it as a package? Is there a document that I can follow for this?
Thank you!

item.title: How can I add Frontity to an existing react project?
item.dc:creator: luisherranz
item.description:
mburridge:

@luisherranz can you help?

What you said is correct

Read full topic
item.source: How can I add Frontity to an existing react project?

item.title: How can I add Frontity to an existing react project?
item.dc:creator: mburridge
item.description:
Hi @pushkar.rulez

Welcome to the Frontity community.

Frontity is a framework that uses React. In this respect it is similar to Gatsby or Next. As such I don’t think that you can retro-fit Frontity to an existing React project. Like Gatsby or Next, Frontity assumes that you are using it as your starting point.

That said, I’ll refer this to a member of our development team in case they have further insights on this.

@luisherranz can you help?

Read full topic
item.source: How can I add Frontity to an existing react project?

item.title: How can I add Frontity to an existing react project?
item.dc:creator: pushkar.rulez
item.description:
I have developed a decent sized react project already for my website. All the documentation that I have found so far, assumes that I’m creating my react project with Frontity. Would it be possible to add Frontity framework to it by installing it as a package? Is there a document that I can follow for this?

Thank you!

Read full topic
